title: Dark Theme Fix — Wave 148 Screens
description: Replace all hardcoded light-mode colors in the 6 wave-148 mobile screens with useThemeStyles() from the existing theme system. Currently dark mode shows white/light screens because all colors are hardcoded (#f3f4f6, #ffffff, #111827, etc). The theme system (useThemeStyles hook, colors.ts) already provides dark/light variants — screens just need to use them. 106 hardcoded color values across 6 files.

modifications.description: Replace 20 hardcoded color values with useThemeStyles() colors. Import useThemeStyles from '../theme'. Add const { colors, isDark, styles } = useThemeStyles(). Replace #f3f4f6→colors.background, #ffffff→colors.card, #111827→colors.text, #6b7280→colors.textSecondary, #1e40af→colors.primary, #e5e7eb→colors.border, #d1d5db→colors.inputBorder, #9ca3af→colors.placeholder.
Replace 17 hardcoded color values with useThemeStyles() colors. Same pattern as PersonalInfoScreen.
Replace 16 hardcoded color values with useThemeStyles() colors. Same pattern.
Replace 21 hardcoded color values with useThemeStyles() colors. Same pattern.
Replace 14 hardcoded color values with useThemeStyles() colors. Same pattern.
Replace 18 hardcoded color values with useThemeStyles() colors. Same pattern.

doNotTouch.reason: Theme color definitions — source of truth for light/dark values. No changes needed.
Theme hook — already provides everything needed. No changes.
Reference screen that correctly uses useThemeStyles. Must not regress.
Reference screen that correctly uses useThemeStyles. Must not regress.
Menu screen — not part of this wave's theme fix.
Navigator — not part of this wave.
Wave 148 sync fix — not part of this wave.

integrationChecks.description: N/A — no API changes. Visual-only wave.
All 6 screens still navigate correctly from ProfileScreen menu items. Dark and light mode both render readable text on correct backgrounds.
Toggle dark mode in Settings → navigate to each of the 6 screens → verify text is readable (not dark-on-dark or light-on-light). Toggle back to light → verify screens still look correct.
If useTheme() somehow returns undefined isDark, getThemeColors(false) returns light colors as safe default.
N/A — no feature flags in this wave.

rollbackPlan.immediate: Revert all 6 screen files to pre-wave-149 versions. Colors go back to hardcoded light mode.
rollbackPlan.safeFallback: Screens display in light mode only (current broken state). No data loss.
rollbackPlan.dataImpact: None — purely visual changes, no schema or data modifications.

selfCheck.step: Toggle dark mode on phone. Navigate to all 6 screens. Verify text readability and color consistency.

delegation.description: All 6 screens follow the identical pattern: import useThemeStyles, destructure colors, replace hardcoded values. Can be done by one polecat or split across two.
mayorDirect: Phase 2 TEST-DESIGN: write theme verification tests

polecatMolecules.title: Theme integration for all 6 screens
polecatMolecules.description: For each screen: (1) import { useThemeStyles } from '../theme', (2) add const { colors, isDark, styles } = useThemeStyles() at top of component, (3) replace every hardcoded color with the matching colors.* value, (4) use styles.container/styles.card where applicable to reduce boilerplate. Reference SettingsScreen.tsx lines 41+95 for the correct pattern.
polecatMolecules.styleReference: SettingsScreen.tsx (line 41: import, line 95: destructure, lines 100+: colors.background/colors.card/colors.text usage). TruckInventoryScreen.tsx (line 22: import, line 37: destructure).

notes: Mobile-only wave. No API, web, or schema changes. Purely visual — replacing hardcoded colors with theme-aware values. EXPO RELOAD MANDATORY in Phase 4 (Rule 18) to verify dark mode on device.
